title: Tools, Device Commands, PBQs, and Certification Capstone · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: Use software, hardware, discovery, capture, and device evidence to design, implement, secure, operate, and repair a branch network. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a performance-based branch network capstone. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use ping, traceroute or tracert, nslookup, dig, netstat, ip, ifconfig, ipconfig, arp, and tcpdump with platform-aware interpretation.
Use protocol analyzers and authorized Nmap discovery or service probes with scope, timing, vantage, and false-result awareness.
Use LLDP or CDP and speed tests while accounting for device support, advertisement trust, test path, server, and competing load.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure
steps.instruction:
Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed quiz case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against n10-009 objectives.

content.text: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: network tools capstone. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Net Certification Evidence, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a complete first version of a performance-based branch network capstone while every earlier competency remains active.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Memorizing acronyms and port numbers demonstrates complete Network Plus readiness. This fails the Net Certification Evidence contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: IEEE 802.3 Ethernet Standard (IEEE 802.3-2022) — Controls Ethernet media, link, speed, duplex, and physical-layer terminology.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Certification Evidence applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Defend a complete design, implementation, operations, security, recovery, and troubleshooting result against N10-009 objectives.
